summary: My name is Rodrigo Botero, and I am originally from Cali, Colombia. I studied topographic engineering and have worked mostly as a teacher and university instructor. Currently, I am finishing my master's degree at the Universidad Javeriana and applying to the PhD program in mathematics at the University of Minnesota. I moved here with my family because most of my relatives live in Minnesota, and I appreciate the peaceful environment and kind people here.

Cali is my hometown in Colombia, a small and warm city where I lived almost all my life. I find it very green and peaceful, which reminds me of Minnesota where I live now. Growing up in Cali shaped my early education and career path before I moved abroad to continue my studies.

Colombia is where I was born and where I started my education and career. Despite having PhD programs in mathematics, I found it difficult to pursue one there at my age of 45 due to systemic problems and closed doors. I hope to return to Colombia in the future with my PhD to work in research and contribute to the country’s academic environment.

I began my university education at the Pontificia Universidad Javeriana, a private institution where I am currently finishing my master's degree in computer systems. This university has been a significant part of my academic journey, and I am working on my thesis there while preparing to apply for a PhD abroad.

I attended Universidad del Valle where I graduated with a major in topographic engineering. This was my first time entering a public university, and it marked the beginning of my professional career. After graduating, I worked briefly as a topographer but mainly focused on teaching and university instruction.

The University of Minnesota is where I am applying to pursue my PhD in mathematics. I was drawn to this university because of its great reputation, the peaceful city environment, and because most of my family lives here. I am currently studying English to meet the program's requirements and am waiting for a decision on my application.

Pursuing a PhD in mathematics is my current academic goal, and I am applying to the program at the University of Minnesota. In Colombia, obtaining a PhD at my age of 45 is nearly impossible due to systemic barriers. I hope to complete the PhD here and eventually return to Colombia to work in research and contribute to the academic community.
